Women Swimmin' for Hospicare · 2026

Twenty years ago,
in the middle of February
in upstate New York

A story of learning, love, and Cayuga Lake

Scroll to read

I taught myself how to swim—via VHS tape.

∿ ∿ ∿

It wasn't pretty, especially when I found myself sharing a lane with actual competitive swimmers while I was just trying to learn how to float.

∿ ∿ ∿

I learned to swim for two reasons: to participate in Women Swimmin', a fundraiser for our local hospice,

and to remember and honor my grandmother.

∿ ∿ ∿

After a brave battle with cancer, home hospice helped her leave us peacefully.

Growing up, my favorite week of the year was spent with her.

She lived by the water but didn't know how to swim.

I knew how to swim enough to get to a ladder to get out of the water — but whenever I was outside, she'd watch me closely from the window, binoculars in hand.


She always said she was spying on the neighbors, but I knew she was keeping her eye on me.

∿ ∿ ∿

Thanks to the crusty VHS tape—

"You don't have to worry anymore, Grandma."

20
In 2026, I will complete my 20th consecutive swim across Cayuga Lake.

To celebrate this milestone,
my goal is to raise

$20,000

in her honor, and in support of the incredible care hospice provides to individuals and families at the end of life — focusing on comfort, dignity, and quality of life.

If you're able to donate or share this message,
I would be deeply grateful.

Every contribution honors her memory and supports families facing end-of-life care.

Donate to Women Swimmin' 2026 →
Thank you — with all my heart — for your kindness and support, Kelly
